status: PLACEHOLDER — awaiting Colorado Springs Utilities standalone financial statements
note_to_populator: This file is a typed scaffold. When the CSU standalone financials arrive, populate each null with the corresponding value. ACFR-consolidated values are shown for comparison/validation. Populate 'last_updated' when filled.

source_when_populated.expected_source: Colorado Springs Utilities standalone financial statements
source_when_populated.obtain_from: Colorado Springs Utilities, Accounting Department, P.O. Box 1103, Mail Code 929, Colorado Springs, CO 80947-0929
source_when_populated.web_location: Typically published at coloradosprings.gov/utilities or cspringsutilities.com
source_when_populated.referenced_in_acfr_note: 2024 Colorado Springs ACFR, Note III.A.2 'Blended Component Units'

consolidated_validation_check_2024.formula_to_verify: electric.operating_revenue + natural_gas.operating_revenue + water.operating_revenue + wastewater.operating_revenue + streetlight.operating_revenue SHOULD EQUAL 1012680000 (within immaterial rounding)

electric.note_on_decommissioning: Martin Drake plant retired 2023; Ray Nixon slated for 2030 decommissioning per Sustainable Energy Plan. 2020 ACFR recorded ~$67.3M impairment associated with early retirement decision.

natural_gas.note_on_pace: PACE (Public Authority for Colorado Energy) is a separate blended component unit providing ~20% of Utilities' natural gas supply under a 30-year prepaid gas contract (2008 issuance). Its revenues pass through to Utilities.

streetlight.note: Typically combined with electric service; may not appear as a separate segment

debt_summary_from_acfr.note: Full issue-by-issue list in bonds.json under fund='utilities'

opeb_plan_summary_from_acfr.note: Shrinking liability as closed group retires. Full trajectory in pension_opeb.json.

PACE_Public_Authority_for_Colorado_Energ.placeholder_note: Populate from CSU standalone or from 2024 ACFR Notes

Young_Gas_Storage_Company_Ltd.purpose: Natural gas storage near Fort Morgan, CO
